title: thymus pulegioides subsp. chamaedrys
intro: Thymus pulegioides subsp. chamaedrys, also known as Wild Thyme or Creeping Thyme, is a low-growing, spreading herb native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a popular remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Wild Thyme include relief from respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce anxiety. Wild Thyme can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil, and it can also be used in cooking and as a garnish in various dishes.
